title: WorkflowState
description: Runtime state for the current Harness Engineering workflow. Carries only runtime state, not task lists, historical logs, or plan body content.

workflowId.description: Unique workflow identifier; immutable after creation. Task-level granularity: one workflow corresponds to one complete request-to-completion-to-archive cycle. L0/L1 work uses this directly as the audit anchor.

activePlanRef.description: Relative path reference to the current plan; null for no-plan work such as L0/L1. Stores only the path, not plan content.

activeTaskId.description: The currently active unique task ID. Required only for plan-driven workflows (L2/L3); L0/L1 no-plan workflows use workflowId as the anchor and keep this field null. Actual existence is checked by validate-state.py against tasks.json.

workflowStatus.description: Overall workflow status, not the status of an individual task.

ownerRole.description: Responsible role for the current workflow gate. L0/L1 workflows without tasks.json use this field to express current ownership; L2/L3 workflows with an active task must align it with the current task.ownerRole.

nextAction.description: The single currently executable next step, expressed as one atomic sentence. Multi-step plans and high-level goals are forbidden.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest state change, ISO 8601 with timezone.

allOf.$comment: The planning phase is owned by the planner
The implementing phase is owned by the developer
The testing phase is owned by the tester
The reviewing phase is owned by the reviewer
The archiving phase is owned by the developer for archive actions
The planning phase must not have an active task
The archiving phase must not hold an active task either
Plan-driven workflows (L2/L3) must have an active task during implementing/testing/reviewing; L0/L1 workflows (activePlanRef=null) may keep activeTaskId null and use workflowId as the anchor
A direct workflow with no active plan must not hold activeTaskId
An active workflow in planning or archiving must bind an active plan package; L0/L1 workflows do not enter these phases
Completed or archived workflows must not have an active plan or active task
The completed state preserves the final L0/L1 review gate shape
Archived workflows must keep currentPhase as archiving
